The road to war was often paved in blood, loss, and regret...

Robb Stark, the new king of The North, was becoming well aware of that act.

What had started as a journey southward to demand the Lannisters release his father, Eddard Stark, from his wrongful charge of treason, turned into an affair for revenge against the new king who beheaded him. Robb was going to drive his sword into King Joffrey for killing his father, even if he died trying.

But he'd have to fight his way to King's Landing first...Tywin Lannister was definitely not making the journey south easy for him and his northern armies, causing every battle along the way to make the company stop to recover and regroup. While true, northern men fought harder and more savagely, southern men fought like soldiers with their extensive military training.

Then there was the parasite names Jaime Lannister...The man who had Robb's father imprisoned in the first place...Part of Robb wanted to just let his wolf Grey Wind have at him, until there was nothing left but bones to send back to King's Landing...But Robb knew that Jaime was more useful alive, if only to be traded for the safe return of his two sisters who were practically being held captive in the walls of the Red Keep. Now that same parasite is Robb's prisoner...

This war would be the death of him, Robb just knew it...

After another grueling day as leader of this Northern company, the only thing on Robb's mind was to strip off all the layers of his thick winter armor and lay in his bedding. To dream that he never had to make this journey in the first place, that he could come back home to Winterfell and find his father there waiting for him.

When he made it to the safe heaven of his tent, Rob began to shed off each piece of his armor and cloak with a sigh, lazily sliding a robe over his shoulders and tying it even lazier. As he sat down at the table with a soft huff, Robb's eyes looked across the marked map of Westeros with almost distaste, glaring at each wooden piece that represented a house that sided with his enemy.

With a heavy groan, Robb leaned back in the wooden chair and tilted his head back to let his eyes flutter closed. Never in his life had he wanted to return home more...Where he could spar with his brothers and lose himself in the welcoming heat of the brothels...
